title: An Israelite Residency at Mahanaim in Transjordan?
author: Israel Finkelstein; Tallay Ornan
publisher: Tel Aviv: Journal of the Institute of Archaeology of Tel Aviv University, vol. 51
allowedUseNotes: Copyrighted journal article. Project stores its own summary of the site-identification proposal only; no reproduction of the article's photographs or drawings of the carved ashlar blocks.
summary: Reports roughly ten incised ashlar blocks depicting lions and a banquet scene, found at Tulul adh-Dhahab al-Gharbi (the western of two mounds flanking the Jabbok/Zarqa river in Transjordan), stylistically dated to around the early-to-mid 8th century BCE. Finkelstein and Ornan interpret the blocks as remains of an Israelite-period monumental building and tentatively propose identifying the site with biblical Mahanaim, connecting it to Israelite control of the region under the Omride and later Jehu/Nimshide dynasties. The article's own title frames this as a question/proposal, not a settled identification; other Jabbok-valley sites (e.g., Tell Hajjaj, Khirbet Mahneh) remain competing candidates in the wider historical-geography literature.
confidenceNotes: Seeded from secondary press coverage (Jerusalem Post, Haaretz, Biblical Archaeology Society reporting on the article) and the article's own title/abstract as surfaced in search, not from a full read of the Tel Aviv journal article itself. TO VERIFY: page-level detail on the argument's strength and Finkelstein & Ornan's own stated confidence level once the article is consulted directly.
